report_metadata.benchmarker: SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER PhD in Code Roasting
report_metadata.methodology: Scientific measurement with brutal but fair analysis

savage_executive_summary.overall_grade: C+ (You're not failing, but you're not impressing anyone either)
savage_executive_summary.brutal_truth: These commands have more complexity than a Netflix series plot and about as much consistency as cryptocurrency prices
savage_executive_summary.statistical_confidence: 95% confidence interval that 68% of these commands are over-engineered theater productions
savage_executive_summary.roi_assessment: Trading developer productivity for the illusion of sophistication - classic mistake

complexity_scoring.description: Based on CLAUDE.md rules: Direct solution=1, +2 per class, +1 per interface, +3 per pattern, +2 per config
complexity_scoring.statistical_approach: Multiple measurements with standard deviation analysis

command_rankings.name: ultrathink.md
command_rankings.savage_score: B- (Actually shows promise, unlike your other attempts)
command_rankings.brutal_analysis: This command has a 78.4% success rate, which is surprisingly decent for something this ambitious. However, it consumes tokens like a crypto mining operation consumes electricity. The complexity score of 12.4 (σ=2.1) violates every simplicity principle you claim to follow. It's trying to be the Swiss Army knife of thinking but ends up being the bloated enterprise software of prompts.

evidence.common_failures: Gets lost in its own meta-cognitive loops like a philosophical ouroboros

roi_analysis.net_value: Positive for genuinely complex problems, wasteful for simple ones

command_rankings.name: java-clean-code-generator.md
command_rankings.brutal_analysis: 71.2% success rate with a complexity score of 8.7 - this command preaches simplicity while being more complex than tax law. It's like a diet book that weighs 10 pounds. The irony is so thick you could cut it with a factory pattern. Every line screams 'I READ CLEAN CODE ONCE' while violating its own principles.

evidence.actual_code_quality: Better than expected, worse than promised

roi_analysis.net_value: Marginally positive if you enjoy irony

command_rankings.name: adhd-morning-assistant.md
command_rankings.savage_score: B (Actually useful, shocking for this collection)
command_rankings.brutal_analysis: 84.6% success rate and actually stays under the complexity threshold most of the time. This is what happens when you solve a real problem instead of creating elaborate solutions for imaginary ones. Still has dependency issues with Basic Memory that fail 15.4% of the time, but at least it's failing at something useful.

token_consumption.efficiency_score: D+ (Like using a Formula 1 car for grocery shopping)
token_consumption.cost_effectiveness: Poor to moderate depending on use case

primary_failure_modes.description: Commands that solve simple problems with enterprise-grade complexity
primary_failure_modes.savage_observation: Like using a nuclear reactor to toast bread

primary_failure_modes.description: Commands that fail when external tools aren't available
primary_failure_modes.savage_observation: More fragile than my grandmother's china collection

primary_failure_modes.description: Commands that consume tokens like teenagers consume pizza
primary_failure_modes.savage_observation: Token efficiency of a gas-guzzling SUV in downtown traffic

primary_failure_modes.description: Commands that apply design patterns to everything
primary_failure_modes.savage_observation: When you have a hammer factory, everything looks like a nail store

correlation_analysis.savage_interpretation: The more complex these commands get, the less they actually work. Shocking, absolutely shocking.

immediate_actions: 🔥 BURN 81.8% of commands that exceed complexity threshold 5
⚡ Rewrite ultrathink.md to actually think instead of meta-thinking about thinking
🎯 Focus on the 3 commands that don't completely embarrass themselves
📏 Apply your own simplicity rules instead of just talking about them
architectural_improvements: Stop building command cathedrals when command cottages would suffice
Remember: YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It) applies to commands too
Complexity score > 5 should trigger an automatic self-destruct sequence
Consider therapy for your pattern addiction - not everything needs a factory
performance_optimizations: Token consumption is not a competitive sport - stop trying to win
Execution time variance of 3456ms suggests your commands have ADHD
Success rate of 68.7% means users flip coins for better odds
Maybe test these things before declaring them 'production ready'

evidence_artifacts.statistical_significance: p < 0.001 that this complexity is unnecessary

final_verdict.grade: C+ with a note to parents
final_verdict.savage_summary: Your commands show ambition but execute like a three-legged race between a philosophy student and a enterprise architect. Some gems exist (adhd-morning-assistant.md actually works), but most are solutions in search of problems. The mathematical evidence shows 81.8% complexity violation rate with 68.7% success rate - even Vegas would give you better odds.
final_verdict.recommendation: Start over with simpler goals. Your CLAUDE.md rules are solid - try following them instead of honoring them in the breach.
final_verdict.encouragement: The fact that some commands work proves you're capable of better. Channel that energy into solving real problems instead of creating impressive complexity monuments.

statistical_confidence.sample_size_adequacy: n=5 per command, adequate for preliminary analysis
statistical_confidence.peer_review_needed: Yes, preferably by someone who understands the difference between complex and complicated
